title: BAI Implements 3x15 Scoring System for Junior Domestic Tournaments
indian_angle: This new scoring system will be implemented across all domestic ranking tournaments and national championships for junior categories in India.
summary: The Badminton Association of India is introducing a 3x15 scoring system for U-13, U-15, and U-17 domestic ranking tournaments and national championships, starting with the Ernakulam event.

title: Kidambi Srikanth Finishes Runner-Up at US Open
indian_angle: Indian shuttler Kidambi Srikanth reached the final of the US Open Super 300 tournament, showcasing his return to top form.
summary: Kidambi Srikanth's impressive run at the US Open came to an end in the final, where he was defeated by Su Li Yang, securing a runner-up finish.

title: Rounak Chouhan Stuns World No. 6 Chou Tien Chen at US Open
indian_angle: Young Indian shuttler Rounak Chouhan achieved a significant upset by defeating World No. 6 Chou Tien Chen at the US Open.
summary: Indian shuttler Rounak Chouhan, a Cristiano Ronaldo fan, caused a major upset at the US Open Super 300 by beating the higher-ranked Chou Tien Chen.

title: All India Senior Ranking Tournament Scheduled for Chennai in July
indian_angle: This circular announces a major All India Senior Ranking Badminton Tournament to be held in Chennai, crucial for Indian players' rankings.
summary: The Badminton Association of India has issued a circular for the Yonex-Sunrise All India Senior Ranking Badminton Tournament 2026, scheduled from July 16th to 23rd in Chennai, Tamil Nadu.

title: India's Asian Games Badminton Squad Reassembles, Treesa-Gayatri Return
indian_angle: The Indian Thomas Cup squad is reassembling, with the notable return of Treesa Jolly and Gayatri Gopichand for the upcoming Asian Games.
summary: India's badminton squad for the Asian Games is taking shape, with key players from the Thomas Cup team and the return of the women's doubles pair Treesa Jolly and Gayatri Gopichand.

title: Satwik-Chirag Crowned Singapore Open Champions After Thrilling Final
indian_angle: India's top men's doubles pair, Satwiksairaj Rankireddy and Chirag Shetty, clinched the prestigious Singapore Open title.
summary: Satwiksairaj Rankireddy and Chirag Shetty secured the Singapore Open title after a thrilling final, displaying exceptional skill and their signature celebratory flair.
